title: General government final consumption expenditure (% of GDP)
definition: Final consumption expenditure is expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. General government FCE includes all government current expenditures for purchases of goods and services (including compensation of employees), and most expenditures on national defense and security, but excludes government military expenditures that are part of government capital formation. This indicator is expressed as a percentage of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) which is the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period.

editorial.shortHook: A candidate map for government consumption.
editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Ukraine, Lesotho, Equatorial Guinea, while the lightest include Egypt, Arab Rep., Haiti, Cambodia; that contrast is the fastest way to read the government consumption pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Government consumption may add useful WORLDPRINT variety once source coverage, unit clarity, map interest, and distractor ambiguity are reviewed.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Government revenue excluding grants can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Economic-share maps are not the same as raw totals.
Small open economies can dominate percentage maps.
